zh.lifeSummary: 當Sarah Isabel Wingfield於1472年出生在英國巴金哈姆爾其勒時，她的父親Roger Wingfield22歲，母親Margaret Dorwood22歲。她於1491年與John Mauncell在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1533年享年61歲。

nameMeanings.name: Wingfield
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Winfield
meanings: English: habitational name from Wingfield in Derbyshire, Bedfordshire, Suffolk, and Wiltshire. The Derbyshire placename probably derives from Old English winn ‘pasture, meadow’ + feld ‘open country’. The Bedfordshire placename may derive from Old English wince ‘nook, corner’ + feld. The Suffolk placename may derive from the Old English personal name Wiga + the Old English groupname suffix -ingas (genitive -inga-) or Old English wīg ‘idol’ + feld. The Wiltshire placename derives from the Old English personal name Wina + Old English feld.

nameMeanings.name: Sarah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the wife of Abraham and mother of Isaac. According to the Book of Genesis, she was originally called Sarai (possibly meaning ‘contentious’ in Hebrew), but had her name changed by God to the more auspicious Sarah ‘princess’ in token of a greater blessing (Genesis 17:15, ‘And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall her name be’). This has been one of the most enduringly popular girls' names.

variants.name: Sara
meanings: Variant of   Sarah .  This is the form used in the Greek of the New Testament (Hebrews 11:11).

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

en.lifeSummary: When Sarah Isabel Wingfield was born in 1472, in Chicheley, Buckinghamshire, England, her father, Roger Wingfield, was 22 and her mother, Margaret Dorwood, was 22. She married John Mauncell in 1491, in Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. She died in 1533, at the age of 61.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sarah Isabel Wingfield est née en 1472, à Chicheley, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, son père, Roger Wingfield, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Margaret Dorwood, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé John Mauncell en 1491, à Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en 1533, à l’âge de 61 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sarah Isabel Wingfield 1472 in Chicheley, Buckinghamshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Roger Wingfield 22 und ihre Mutter Margaret Dorwood 22. Sie hat 1491 in Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom John Mauncell geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter. Sie starb 1533 im Alter von 61 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sarah Isabel Wingfield è nata il nel 1472 nel Chicheley, Buckinghamshire, England, sua padre, Roger Wingfield, aveva 22 anni e sua madre, Margaret Dorwood, aveva 22 anni. Lei sposato John Mauncell nel 1491, nel Buckinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 3 figlie. Morì nel 1533, all'età di 61 anni.
